he.text: Arjuna said O Janārdan O Keśava if You consider חוכמה superior to action why then do you urge me to engage in the violence of warfare

ar.text: Arjuna said O Janārdan O Keśava if You consider الحكمة superior to action why then do you urge me to engage in the violence of warfare

tr.text: Arjuna said O Janārdan O Keśava if You consider bilgelik superior to action why then do you urge me to engage in the violence of warfare

sw.text: Arjuna said O Janārdan O Keśava if You consider busara superior to action why then do you urge me to engage in the violence of warfare

he.text: I am confused by Your words They appear to be ambiguous sometimes supporting action and sometimes supporting חוכמה So please tell me which is the most beneficial path for me to take

ar.text: I am confused by Your words They appear to be ambiguous sometimes supporting action and sometimes supporting الحكمة So please tell me which is the most beneficial path for me to take

tr.text: I am confused by Your words They appear to be ambiguous sometimes supporting action and sometimes supporting bilgelik So please tell me which is the most beneficial path for me to take

sw.text: I am confused by Your words They appear to be ambiguous sometimes supporting action and sometimes supporting busara So please tell me which is the most beneficial path for me to take

he.text: The Supreme Lord replied O sinless one I have described the two paths to be found in this world—the yoga of חוכמה practised by the realised and the yoga of selfless action practised by those active in the world Both paths touch the beginnings of devotional practice since the ladder to the plane of מסירות is one while the practice is twofold according to the stage attained

ar.text: The Supreme Lord replied O sinless one I have described the two paths to be found in this world—the yoga of الحكمة practised by the realised and the yoga of selfless action practised by those active in the world Both paths touch the beginnings of devotional practice since the ladder to the plane of التفاني is one while the practice is twofold according to the stage attained

tr.text: The Supreme Lord replied O sinless one I have described the two paths to be found in this world—the yoga of bilgelik practised by the realised and the yoga of selfless action practised by those active in the world Both paths touch the beginnings of devotional practice since the ladder to the plane of bağlılık is one while the practice is twofold according to the stage attained

sw.text: The Supreme Lord replied O sinless one I have described the two paths to be found in this world—the yoga of busara practised by the realised and the yoga of selfless action practised by those active in the world Both paths touch the beginnings of devotional practice since the ladder to the plane of ujitoaji is one while the practice is twofold according to the stage attained

he.text: One cannot attain freedom from action by refraining from action Nor can one attain fulfillment by renunciation alone

ar.text: One cannot attain freedom from action by refraining from action Nor can one attain fulfillment by renunciation alone

tr.text: One cannot attain freedom from action by refraining from action Nor can one attain fulfillment by renunciation alone

sw.text: One cannot attain freedom from action by refraining from action Nor can one attain fulfillment by renunciation alone

he.text: No one can refrain from activity for even a moment Everyone is impelled to act by the modes of material nature Thus the person of impure consciousness should not disregard scripturally prescribed purificatory works

ar.text: No one can refrain from activity for even a moment Everyone is impelled to act by the modes of material nature Thus the person of impure consciousness should not disregard scripturally prescribed purificatory works

tr.text: No one can refrain from activity for even a moment Everyone is impelled to act by the modes of material nature Thus the person of impure consciousness should not disregard scripturally prescribed purificatory works

sw.text: No one can refrain from activity for even a moment Everyone is impelled to act by the modes of material nature Thus the person of impure consciousness should not disregard scripturally prescribed purificatory works

he.text: One who externally restrains his hands legs and other senses of action but whose mind dwells on sense objects is a fool Know him to be a hypocrite

ar.text: One who externally restrains his hands legs and other senses of action but whose mind dwells on sense objects is a fool Know him to be a hypocrite

tr.text: One who externally restrains his hands legs and other senses of action but whose mind dwells on sense objects is a fool Know him to be a hypocrite

sw.text: One who externally restrains his hands legs and other senses of action but whose mind dwells on sense objects is a fool Know him to be a hypocrite

he.text: O Arjuna superior is one who though married has stabilised his senses by the mind and engaged in the yoga of selfless action

ar.text: O Arjuna superior is one who though married has stabilised his senses by the mind and engaged in the yoga of selfless action

tr.text: O Arjuna superior is one who though married has stabilised his senses by the mind and engaged in the yoga of selfless action

sw.text: O Arjuna superior is one who though married has stabilised his senses by the mind and engaged in the yoga of selfless action

he.text: Perform your prescribed duties since to be active is better than idleness Inactive persons cannot even maintain their existence By giving up fruitive action and performing your obligatory duties your heart will be purified Surpassing the plane of renunciation you will attain pure מסירות transcendental to the mundane plane

ar.text: Perform your prescribed duties since to be active is better than idleness Inactive persons cannot even maintain their existence By giving up fruitive action and performing your obligatory duties your heart will be purified Surpassing the plane of renunciation you will attain pure التفاني transcendental to the mundane plane

tr.text: Perform your prescribed duties since to be active is better than idleness Inactive persons cannot even maintain their existence By giving up fruitive action and performing your obligatory duties your heart will be purified Surpassing the plane of renunciation you will attain pure bağlılık transcendental to the mundane plane

sw.text: Perform your prescribed duties since to be active is better than idleness Inactive persons cannot even maintain their existence By giving up fruitive action and performing your obligatory duties your heart will be purified Surpassing the plane of renunciation you will attain pure ujitoaji transcendental to the mundane plane

he.text: Selfless duty performed as an offering to the Supreme Lord is called yajña or sacrifice O Arjuna all action performed for any other purpose is the cause of bondage in this world of repeated birth and death Therefore remaining unattached to the fruits of action perform all your duties in the spirit of such sacrifice Such selfless action is the way to begin devotional practice gradually realise the presence of the Lord and ultimately attain pure מסירות transcendental to the modes of material nature [nirguṇa bhakti]

ar.text: Selfless duty performed as an offering to the Supreme Lord is called yajña or sacrifice O Arjuna all action performed for any other purpose is the cause of bondage in this world of repeated birth and death Therefore remaining unattached to the fruits of action perform all your duties in the spirit of such sacrifice Such selfless action is the way to begin devotional practice gradually realise the presence of the Lord and ultimately attain pure التفاني transcendental to the modes of material nature [nirguṇa bhakti]

tr.text: Selfless duty performed as an offering to the Supreme Lord is called yajña or sacrifice O Arjuna all action performed for any other purpose is the cause of bondage in this world of repeated birth and death Therefore remaining unattached to the fruits of action perform all your duties in the spirit of such sacrifice Such selfless action is the way to begin devotional practice gradually realise the presence of the Lord and ultimately attain pure bağlılık transcendental to the modes of material nature [nirguṇa bhakti]

sw.text: Selfless duty performed as an offering to the Supreme Lord is called yajña or sacrifice O Arjuna all action performed for any other purpose is the cause of bondage in this world of repeated birth and death Therefore remaining unattached to the fruits of action perform all your duties in the spirit of such sacrifice Such selfless action is the way to begin devotional practice gradually realise the presence of the Lord and ultimately attain pure ujitoaji transcendental to the modes of material nature [nirguṇa bhakti]

he.text: In the beginning Lord Brahmā manifested the progeny of the world along with sacrifices He instructed them thus “Take shelter of this religious principle of sacrifice prosper and flourish May such sacrifice be the bestower of the things you desire.”

ar.text: In the beginning Lord Brahmā manifested the progeny of the world along with sacrifices He instructed them thus “Take shelter of this religious principle of sacrifice prosper and flourish May such sacrifice be the bestower of the things you desire.”

tr.text: In the beginning Lord Brahmā manifested the progeny of the world along with sacrifices He instructed them thus “Take shelter of this religious principle of sacrifice prosper and flourish May such sacrifice be the bestower of the things you desire.”

sw.text: In the beginning Lord Brahmā manifested the progeny of the world along with sacrifices He instructed them thus “Take shelter of this religious principle of sacrifice prosper and flourish May such sacrifice be the bestower of the things you desire.”

he.text: “Propitiate the gods by sacrifices and may they satisfy you by bestowing your desired fruits In this way through mutual nourishment you will be the gainers of great auspiciousness.”

ar.text: “Propitiate the gods by sacrifices and may they satisfy you by bestowing your desired fruits In this way through mutual nourishment you will be the gainers of great auspiciousness.”

tr.text: “Propitiate the gods by sacrifices and may they satisfy you by bestowing your desired fruits In this way through mutual nourishment you will be the gainers of great auspiciousness.”

sw.text: “Propitiate the gods by sacrifices and may they satisfy you by bestowing your desired fruits In this way through mutual nourishment you will be the gainers of great auspiciousness.”

he.text: Propitiated by sacrifices the gods My worldly representatives will bestow upon you all your desired enjoyable things But one who enjoys what is given by the gods without offering it to them is a thief

ar.text: Propitiated by sacrifices the gods My worldly representatives will bestow upon you all your desired enjoyable things But one who enjoys what is given by the gods without offering it to them is a thief

tr.text: Propitiated by sacrifices the gods My worldly representatives will bestow upon you all your desired enjoyable things But one who enjoys what is given by the gods without offering it to them is a thief

sw.text: Propitiated by sacrifices the gods My worldly representatives will bestow upon you all your desired enjoyable things But one who enjoys what is given by the gods without offering it to them is a thief

he.text: By accepting sacrificial remnants of the universal gods* virtuous persons are liberated from all sins arising from violence towards other living entities But those who prepare food for their own pleasure partake only of sin

ar.text: By accepting sacrificial remnants of the universal gods* virtuous persons are liberated from all sins arising from violence towards other living entities But those who prepare food for their own pleasure partake only of sin

tr.text: By accepting sacrificial remnants of the universal gods* virtuous persons are liberated from all sins arising from violence towards other living entities But those who prepare food for their own pleasure partake only of sin

sw.text: By accepting sacrificial remnants of the universal gods* virtuous persons are liberated from all sins arising from violence towards other living entities But those who prepare food for their own pleasure partake only of sin

he.text: The living beings’ bodies are manifest from food food is manifest due to rain rainfall ensues from the performance of sacrifice and action is the cause of sacrifice

ar.text: The living beings’ bodies are manifest from food food is manifest due to rain rainfall ensues from the performance of sacrifice and action is the cause of sacrifice

tr.text: The living beings’ bodies are manifest from food food is manifest due to rain rainfall ensues from the performance of sacrifice and action is the cause of sacrifice

sw.text: The living beings’ bodies are manifest from food food is manifest due to rain rainfall ensues from the performance of sacrifice and action is the cause of sacrifice

he.text: Action is prescribed in the Vedas and the Vedas originate in Akṣara the Imperishable Therefore the all-pervading imperishable Lord is ever present within the acts of sacrifice offered to Him

ar.text: Action is prescribed in the Vedas and the Vedas originate in Akṣara the Imperishable Therefore the all-pervading imperishable Lord is ever present within the acts of sacrifice offered to Him

tr.text: Action is prescribed in the Vedas and the Vedas originate in Akṣara the Imperishable Therefore the all-pervading imperishable Lord is ever present within the acts of sacrifice offered to Him

sw.text: Action is prescribed in the Vedas and the Vedas originate in Akṣara the Imperishable Therefore the all-pervading imperishable Lord is ever present within the acts of sacrifice offered to Him

he.text: O Arjuna one who does not respect this natural causal cycle leads a life of sin enjoying the senses in vain

ar.text: O Arjuna one who does not respect this natural causal cycle leads a life of sin enjoying the senses in vain

tr.text: O Arjuna one who does not respect this natural causal cycle leads a life of sin enjoying the senses in vain

sw.text: O Arjuna one who does not respect this natural causal cycle leads a life of sin enjoying the senses in vain

he.text: Yet there is no duty to fulfill for the realised person who delights within the self being fully satisfied within His only worldly action or karma is as required for his subsistence

ar.text: Yet there is no duty to fulfill for the realised person who delights within the self being fully satisfied within His only worldly action or karma is as required for his subsistence

tr.text: Yet there is no duty to fulfill for the realised person who delights within the self being fully satisfied within His only worldly action or karma is as required for his subsistence

sw.text: Yet there is no duty to fulfill for the realised person who delights within the self being fully satisfied within His only worldly action or karma is as required for his subsistence

he.text: In the world he neither gains by action nor loses by inaction He does not depend on any other being from Lord Brahmā down to the simplest life-form for any purpose

ar.text: In the world he neither gains by action nor loses by inaction He does not depend on any other being from Lord Brahmā down to the simplest life-form for any purpose

tr.text: In the world he neither gains by action nor loses by inaction He does not depend on any other being from Lord Brahmā down to the simplest life-form for any purpose

sw.text: In the world he neither gains by action nor loses by inaction He does not depend on any other being from Lord Brahmā down to the simplest life-form for any purpose

he.text: So perform your prescribed duties without attachment By selflessly executing one’s duties a person attains liberation True liberation is the state of pure מסירות attained in the maturity of selfless action

ar.text: So perform your prescribed duties without attachment By selflessly executing one’s duties a person attains liberation True liberation is the state of pure التفاني attained in the maturity of selfless action

tr.text: So perform your prescribed duties without attachment By selflessly executing one’s duties a person attains liberation True liberation is the state of pure bağlılık attained in the maturity of selfless action

sw.text: So perform your prescribed duties without attachment By selflessly executing one’s duties a person attains liberation True liberation is the state of pure ujitoaji attained in the maturity of selfless action

he.text: King Janaka and other learned personalities succeeded in attaining מסירות through the execution of their prescribed duties So it is fitting that you fulfill your duty in consideration of the welfare of the people

ar.text: King Janaka and other learned personalities succeeded in attaining التفاني through the execution of their prescribed duties So it is fitting that you fulfill your duty in consideration of the welfare of the people

tr.text: King Janaka and other learned personalities succeeded in attaining bağlılık through the execution of their prescribed duties So it is fitting that you fulfill your duty in consideration of the welfare of the people

sw.text: King Janaka and other learned personalities succeeded in attaining ujitoaji through the execution of their prescribed duties So it is fitting that you fulfill your duty in consideration of the welfare of the people

he.text: The masses follow the ways of great men following the standards they set

ar.text: The masses follow the ways of great men following the standards they set

tr.text: The masses follow the ways of great men following the standards they set

sw.text: The masses follow the ways of great men following the standards they set

he.text: O Arjuna I have no duty in the three worlds I lack nothing and there is nothing to be attained by Me and yet I Myself am active

ar.text: O Arjuna I have no duty in the three worlds I lack nothing and there is nothing to be attained by Me and yet I Myself am active

tr.text: O Arjuna I have no duty in the three worlds I lack nothing and there is nothing to be attained by Me and yet I Myself am active

sw.text: O Arjuna I have no duty in the three worlds I lack nothing and there is nothing to be attained by Me and yet I Myself am active

he.text: O Arjuna if ever I do not engage attentively in duties then all men following My example will give up their duties

ar.text: O Arjuna if ever I do not engage attentively in duties then all men following My example will give up their duties

tr.text: O Arjuna if ever I do not engage attentively in duties then all men following My example will give up their duties

sw.text: O Arjuna if ever I do not engage attentively in duties then all men following My example will give up their duties

he.text: If I do not engage in duty the people of these worlds will renounce their duties and fall to ruination Thus I will be responsible for their downfall caused by adulterated progeny

ar.text: If I do not engage in duty the people of these worlds will renounce their duties and fall to ruination Thus I will be responsible for their downfall caused by adulterated progeny

tr.text: If I do not engage in duty the people of these worlds will renounce their duties and fall to ruination Thus I will be responsible for their downfall caused by adulterated progeny

sw.text: If I do not engage in duty the people of these worlds will renounce their duties and fall to ruination Thus I will be responsible for their downfall caused by adulterated progeny

he.text: O Arjuna as ignorant persons work with attachment the wise too must work but without attachment in order to guide the general people who exist in the plane of worldly action They differ not in their work but in their attitudes of attachment and detachment

ar.text: O Arjuna as ignorant persons work with attachment the wise too must work but without attachment in order to guide the general people who exist in the plane of worldly action They differ not in their work but in their attitudes of attachment and detachment

tr.text: O Arjuna as ignorant persons work with attachment the wise too must work but without attachment in order to guide the general people who exist in the plane of worldly action They differ not in their work but in their attitudes of attachment and detachment

sw.text: O Arjuna as ignorant persons work with attachment the wise too must work but without attachment in order to guide the general people who exist in the plane of worldly action They differ not in their work but in their attitudes of attachment and detachment

he.text: The scripturally learned teacher should not create confusion in the minds of ignorant attached men by apparently advocating the neglect of duty for the cultivation of ידע Rather he should set the example of selflessness in fulfilling duties thus encouraging the ignorant in the performance of their prescribed duties

ar.text: The scripturally learned teacher should not create confusion in the minds of ignorant attached men by apparently advocating the neglect of duty for the cultivation of المعرفة Rather he should set the example of selflessness in fulfilling duties thus encouraging the ignorant in the performance of their prescribed duties

tr.text: The scripturally learned teacher should not create confusion in the minds of ignorant attached men by apparently advocating the neglect of duty for the cultivation of bilgi Rather he should set the example of selflessness in fulfilling duties thus encouraging the ignorant in the performance of their prescribed duties

sw.text: The scripturally learned teacher should not create confusion in the minds of ignorant attached men by apparently advocating the neglect of duty for the cultivation of ujuzi Rather he should set the example of selflessness in fulfilling duties thus encouraging the ignorant in the performance of their prescribed duties

he.text: All actions in the world are in every respect effected by the modes of material nature which impell the senses But a man deluded by bodily identification thinks “I alone am accomplishing this work.”

ar.text: All actions in the world are in every respect effected by the modes of material nature which impell the senses But a man deluded by bodily identification thinks “I alone am accomplishing this work.”

tr.text: All actions in the world are in every respect effected by the modes of material nature which impell the senses But a man deluded by bodily identification thinks “I alone am accomplishing this work.”

sw.text: All actions in the world are in every respect effected by the modes of material nature which impell the senses But a man deluded by bodily identification thinks “I alone am accomplishing this work.”

he.text: However O mighty-armed Arjuna one who is in ידע of the division of the modes of material nature and action knows that the modes are engaging within the modes and he is detached from them One who knows that the three modes activated by the demigods impell the engagement of the senses with their objects knows himself to be independent of these elements therefore he does not consider himself a doer

ar.text: However O mighty-armed Arjuna one who is in المعرفة of the division of the modes of material nature and action knows that the modes are engaging within the modes and he is detached from them One who knows that the three modes activated by the demigods impell the engagement of the senses with their objects knows himself to be independent of these elements therefore he does not consider himself a doer

tr.text: However O mighty-armed Arjuna one who is in bilgi of the division of the modes of material nature and action knows that the modes are engaging within the modes and he is detached from them One who knows that the three modes activated by the demigods impell the engagement of the senses with their objects knows himself to be independent of these elements therefore he does not consider himself a doer

sw.text: However O mighty-armed Arjuna one who is in ujuzi of the division of the modes of material nature and action knows that the modes are engaging within the modes and he is detached from them One who knows that the three modes activated by the demigods impell the engagement of the senses with their objects knows himself to be independent of these elements therefore he does not consider himself a doer

he.text: Persons bewildered by the modes of nature are absorbed in the functions of the modes the engagement of the senses with their objects Enlightened persons should not agitate such ignorant unfortunate persons by trying to convince them of higher philosophical truths Rather they should advise the conditioned souls to practise selfless action which liberates them from absorption in the modes

ar.text: Persons bewildered by the modes of nature are absorbed in the functions of the modes the engagement of the senses with their objects Enlightened persons should not agitate such ignorant unfortunate persons by trying to convince them of higher philosophical truths Rather they should advise the conditioned souls to practise selfless action which liberates them from absorption in the modes

tr.text: Persons bewildered by the modes of nature are absorbed in the functions of the modes the engagement of the senses with their objects Enlightened persons should not agitate such ignorant unfortunate persons by trying to convince them of higher philosophical truths Rather they should advise the conditioned souls to practise selfless action which liberates them from absorption in the modes

sw.text: Persons bewildered by the modes of nature are absorbed in the functions of the modes the engagement of the senses with their objects Enlightened persons should not agitate such ignorant unfortunate persons by trying to convince them of higher philosophical truths Rather they should advise the conditioned souls to practise selfless action which liberates them from absorption in the modes

he.text: Surrender all your activities to Me with this understanding “All my actions are under the control of the indwelling Lord.” In such consciousness free from possessiveness and anguish take recourse to battle as your natural duty

ar.text: Surrender all your activities to Me with this understanding “All my actions are under the control of the indwelling Lord.” In such consciousness free from possessiveness and anguish take recourse to battle as your natural duty

tr.text: Surrender all your activities to Me with this understanding “All my actions are under the control of the indwelling Lord.” In such consciousness free from possessiveness and anguish take recourse to battle as your natural duty

sw.text: Surrender all your activities to Me with this understanding “All my actions are under the control of the indwelling Lord.” In such consciousness free from possessiveness and anguish take recourse to battle as your natural duty

he.text: Faithful unbegrudging men who always practise My teaching this yoga of selfless action are liberated from the bondage of action even though they engage in action

ar.text: Faithful unbegrudging men who always practise My teaching this yoga of selfless action are liberated from the bondage of action even though they engage in action

tr.text: Faithful unbegrudging men who always practise My teaching this yoga of selfless action are liberated from the bondage of action even though they engage in action

sw.text: Faithful unbegrudging men who always practise My teaching this yoga of selfless action are liberated from the bondage of action even though they engage in action

he.text: But the arrogant who resent My teaching are bereft of all ידע Know them to be lost in ignorance

ar.text: But the arrogant who resent My teaching are bereft of all المعرفة Know them to be lost in ignorance

tr.text: But the arrogant who resent My teaching are bereft of all bilgi Know them to be lost in ignorance

sw.text: But the arrogant who resent My teaching are bereft of all ujuzi Know them to be lost in ignorance

he.text: For even a knowledgable person tends to act according to his nature mundane inclinations The living beings’ endeavours are dictated by their natures What then can repression accomplish

ar.text: For even a knowledgable person tends to act according to his nature mundane inclinations The living beings’ endeavours are dictated by their natures What then can repression accomplish

tr.text: For even a knowledgable person tends to act according to his nature mundane inclinations The living beings’ endeavours are dictated by their natures What then can repression accomplish

sw.text: For even a knowledgable person tends to act according to his nature mundane inclinations The living beings’ endeavours are dictated by their natures What then can repression accomplish

he.text: Although the senses are attracted to and repelled by the sense objects one must not be controlled by these urges for they are the greatest enemy of the candidate for self-realisation

ar.text: Although the senses are attracted to and repelled by the sense objects one must not be controlled by these urges for they are the greatest enemy of the candidate for self-realisation

tr.text: Although the senses are attracted to and repelled by the sense objects one must not be controlled by these urges for they are the greatest enemy of the candidate for self-realisation

sw.text: Although the senses are attracted to and repelled by the sense objects one must not be controlled by these urges for they are the greatest enemy of the candidate for self-realisation

he.text: Even if it is imperfect it is better to do one’s duty according to one’s own nature than to do another’s well Even death in the discharge of one’s own duty is better for to perform another’s is dangerous

ar.text: Even if it is imperfect it is better to do one’s duty according to one’s own nature than to do another’s well Even death in the discharge of one’s own duty is better for to perform another’s is dangerous

tr.text: Even if it is imperfect it is better to do one’s duty according to one’s own nature than to do another’s well Even death in the discharge of one’s own duty is better for to perform another’s is dangerous

sw.text: Even if it is imperfect it is better to do one’s duty according to one’s own nature than to do another’s well Even death in the discharge of one’s own duty is better for to perform another’s is dangerous

he.text: Arjuna inquired O descendant of the Vṛṣṇis by what is the living being compelled to commit sinful activities even unwillingly as if by force

ar.text: Arjuna inquired O descendant of the Vṛṣṇis by what is the living being compelled to commit sinful activities even unwillingly as if by force

tr.text: Arjuna inquired O descendant of the Vṛṣṇis by what is the living being compelled to commit sinful activities even unwillingly as if by force

sw.text: Arjuna inquired O descendant of the Vṛṣṇis by what is the living being compelled to commit sinful activities even unwillingly as if by force

he.text: The Supreme Lord replied It is the desire to enjoy the mundane that induces a person to commit sin and in different situations it produces anger It is utterly insatiable extremely malicious and the worst enemy of the living being in this world

ar.text: The Supreme Lord replied It is the desire to enjoy the mundane that induces a person to commit sin and in different situations it produces anger It is utterly insatiable extremely malicious and the worst enemy of the living being in this world

tr.text: The Supreme Lord replied It is the desire to enjoy the mundane that induces a person to commit sin and in different situations it produces anger It is utterly insatiable extremely malicious and the worst enemy of the living being in this world

sw.text: The Supreme Lord replied It is the desire to enjoy the mundane that induces a person to commit sin and in different situations it produces anger It is utterly insatiable extremely malicious and the worst enemy of the living being in this world

he.text: As fire is thinly veiled by smoke as a mirror is thickly covered with dust and as the embryo remains completely enclosed within the womb similarly this desire covers the consciousness of the living being in these three degrees of intensity according to the modes of material nature—goodness passion and ignorance respectively

ar.text: As fire is thinly veiled by smoke as a mirror is thickly covered with dust and as the embryo remains completely enclosed within the womb similarly this desire covers the consciousness of the living being in these three degrees of intensity according to the modes of material nature—goodness passion and ignorance respectively

tr.text: As fire is thinly veiled by smoke as a mirror is thickly covered with dust and as the embryo remains completely enclosed within the womb similarly this desire covers the consciousness of the living being in these three degrees of intensity according to the modes of material nature—goodness passion and ignorance respectively

sw.text: As fire is thinly veiled by smoke as a mirror is thickly covered with dust and as the embryo remains completely enclosed within the womb similarly this desire covers the consciousness of the living being in these three degrees of intensity according to the modes of material nature—goodness passion and ignorance respectively

he.text: ידע is covered by desire the constant enemy of the wise It burns like an insatiable fire never satisfied by offerings of butter

ar.text: المعرفة is covered by desire the constant enemy of the wise It burns like an insatiable fire never satisfied by offerings of butter

tr.text: bilgi is covered by desire the constant enemy of the wise It burns like an insatiable fire never satisfied by offerings of butter

sw.text: ujuzi is covered by desire the constant enemy of the wise It burns like an insatiable fire never satisfied by offerings of butter

he.text: It is said that this enemy desire dwelling within the senses mind and intelligence covers the perception of the embodied living being and deludes him

ar.text: It is said that this enemy desire dwelling within the senses mind and intelligence covers the perception of the embodied living being and deludes him

tr.text: It is said that this enemy desire dwelling within the senses mind and intelligence covers the perception of the embodied living being and deludes him

sw.text: It is said that this enemy desire dwelling within the senses mind and intelligence covers the perception of the embodied living being and deludes him

he.text: Therefore O noblest of the Bhāratas control your senses and destroy this desire the embodiment of sin which destroys both ידע and realisation

ar.text: Therefore O noblest of the Bhāratas control your senses and destroy this desire the embodiment of sin which destroys both المعرفة and realisation

tr.text: Therefore O noblest of the Bhāratas control your senses and destroy this desire the embodiment of sin which destroys both bilgi and realisation

sw.text: Therefore O noblest of the Bhāratas control your senses and destroy this desire the embodiment of sin which destroys both ujuzi and realisation

he.text: It is said by the wise that the senses are superior to matter the mind is superior to the senses and the intelligence is superior to the mind and the soul is superior to even the intelligence

ar.text: It is said by the wise that the senses are superior to matter the mind is superior to the senses and the intelligence is superior to the mind and the soul is superior to even the intelligence

tr.text: It is said by the wise that the senses are superior to matter the mind is superior to the senses and the intelligence is superior to the mind and the soul is superior to even the intelligence

sw.text: It is said by the wise that the senses are superior to matter the mind is superior to the senses and the intelligence is superior to the mind and the soul is superior to even the intelligence

he.text: O mighty Arjuna knowing the soul to be superior to the intelligence steady the mind with resolute intelligence and destroy the formidable enemy desire

ar.text: O mighty Arjuna knowing the soul to be superior to the intelligence steady the mind with resolute intelligence and destroy the formidable enemy desire

tr.text: O mighty Arjuna knowing the soul to be superior to the intelligence steady the mind with resolute intelligence and destroy the formidable enemy desire

sw.text: O mighty Arjuna knowing the soul to be superior to the intelligence steady the mind with resolute intelligence and destroy the formidable enemy desire
